Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Macy?S West Stores, Inc. To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. STEVEN A. KRONENBERG, Esq. of The Veen Firm, P.C. (Tel: 415-673-4800) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff Brandon Zhao;
Atty. MARK R. WILSON, Esq. of Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (Tel: 415-217-6990) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Macy's West Stores, Inc.

Hearing Re: Defendant Macy's West Stores, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

The motion by Defendant Macy's West Stores, Inc. to strike portions of the first amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs allege that on December 2015, they sustained personal injuries while using a portable butane stove that was purchased from a Macy's store in Stockton, California in the 1990s. (FAC pars. 18, 20.) Plaintiff allege that the stove was defectively designed and seek to state causes of action against Macy's for negligence, strict products liability and breach of warranty. They allege that Macy's was aware of the defective design of the stove, including the risk that it would explode during its normal and intended use, and failed to warn consumers of the risks of harm. These allegatSions are sufficient to support plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages, which are available in a products liability case where the defendant's conduct was despicable and involved conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299-1301 [upholding jury verdict awarding punitive damages against defendant, which knew that its products were likely to pose a danger to users, whom it did not warn].) Defendant's argument that plaintiffs must allege its knowledge with more specificity are unsupported by any authority, and is premature at the pleading stage. (See Civ. Code 3294(a) [plaintiff's burden at trial is to establish oppression, fraud or malice by clear and convincing evidence]; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 59 ["If the plaintiff is to prevail on a claim for punitive damages, the evidence will have to be clear and convincing to the trier of fact."].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
